title: GOING HOME
content:
F       Dm
        In the distant  day was dawning
        Am      B
          Comes to me the       early morning
        Gm      C7     F
        Something tells me      that I'm going  home
        F       Dm
        The brand new sun       shines bright
        Am      B
          From the darkness     fields of light
        Gm      C7     F
        Something tells me      that I'm going  home
 
Refrein:
        Dm      Am
Going   home Where the  summers coming in
        B       F      B      C7
And the         moonlight on the        river   Shows me where I've     been
 
Verse:
        F       Dm
        Soft the rain   gently falling
        Am      B
          Lightly a'cross the   city morning
        Gm      C7     F
          get the feeling       that I'm going  home
        F       Dm
        Across the moorland,    past the mountains
        Am      B
          O'r the rivers be     side the new stream
        Gm      C7     F
        Something tells me      that I'm going  home
 
Refrein:
        Dm      Am
Going   home Where the  summers coming in
        B       F      B      C7
And the         moonlight on the        river   Shows me where I've     been
 
F | Dm | Am | B | Gm | C7 | F 2x
 
Refrein:
        Dm      Am
Going   home Where the  summers coming in
        B       F      B      C7
And the         moonlight on the        river   Shows me where I've     been
 
Verse:
        F       Dm
        As the trains   rolling nearer Ah,
        Am      B
          Ar the feeling        just to be there,
        Gm      C7     F
        Something tells me      that I'm going  home
        F       Dm
        Now the skylines        reach my eyes
        Am      B
          The ridge stands out in       highland skies
        Gm      C7     F
        I just can't be lieve I'm going         home
 
Going home
 
Solo: Gm | C7 | F | % | 4x
